name: Montilla and Nueva Carteya Wine Route
description: This moderate 35.9-mile (57.7 km) road cycling route from Montilla to Nueva Carteya winds through the scenic Montilla-Moriles wine region.
region_intro: Road cycling around Montilla is characterized by a landscape of gentle hills, extensive vineyards, and olive groves that define the Cordoban countryside. The region features a "sea of hills" with rural roads winding through typical Andalusian scenery. The Sierra de Montilla also contributes to the elevated terrain, offering varied gradients for cyclists. This area provides a network of mostly paved surfaces suitable for road cycling. Best road cycling routes around Montilla The most popular road cycling route is Castro del Rio — circular from Montilla, a 33.8 miles (54.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 40 minutes to complete. This route navigates through the region's characteristic gentle hills and agricultural landscapes. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Puente Genil from Montilla — circular via Aguilar de la Frontera, a moderate 39.4 miles (63.4 km) path. It offers views of the expansive Andalusian countryside, passing through rural roads and olive groves. Local road cyclists also love the Montilla and Nueva Carteya Wine Route, a 35.9 miles (57.7 km) trail leading through vineyards and agricultural fields, often completed in about 2 hours 49 minutes. Road cycling around Montilla is defined by vineyards, olive groves, and gentle hills, with rural roads connecting various towns. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, accommodating different ability levels. The routes in Montilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 1000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Montilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Montilla?
A: There are over 70 road cycling routes around Montilla, offering a diverse range of experiences. These include routes for various fitness levels, from moderate to more challenging rides.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling in Montilla?
A: Montilla is characterized by a 'sea of hills' adorned with extensive vineyards and olive groves. You'll find gentle hills and rural roads winding through typical Andalusian countryside, with the Sierra de Montilla adding to the elevated scenery. The routes offer varied gradients, from relaxed tours to more demanding climbs.

Q: What are the typical surface conditions for road cycling routes in Montilla?
A: The road cycling routes in Montilla primarily feature mostly paved surfaces, making them ideal for road bikes. You'll be riding on rural roads that connect various towns and traverse agricultural landscapes.

Q: Are there routes suitable for beginners or families in Montilla?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate or difficult, there are options suitable for less experienced cyclists. The region's gentle hills and rural roads can accommodate easier rides, though specific family-friendly routes might require checking individual tour details for elevation and distance.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Montilla?
A: The best times for road cycling in Montilla are typically spring and autumn. During these seasons, the weather is milder and more pleasant for cycling, allowing you to fully enjoy the vineyards and olive groves without the intense heat of summer.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes starting from Montilla?
A: Yes, Montilla offers several excellent circular road cycling routes. Two popular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008828821/castro-del-rio-circular-from-montilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castro del Rio — circular from Montilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is about 33.8 miles (54.4 km), and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1017189119/puente-genil-from-montilla-circular-via-aguilar-de-la-frontera" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Genil from Montilla — circular via Aguilar de la Frontera&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 39.4 miles (63.4 km) path.

Q: Can I incorporate winery visits into my road cycling trip?
A: Absolutely. The Montilla-Moriles Wine Route is a significant draw, and many cycling paths lead through vineyards. Routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008831066/montilla-and-nueva-carteya-wine-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla and Nueva Carteya Wine Route&lt;/a&gt; offer opportunities for enotourism, allowing you to visit traditional wineries and historic bodegas to taste local wines like Fino, Amontillado, and Pedro Ximénez.

Q: What cultural or historical attractions can I see along the road cycling routes?
A: Beyond the natural beauty, Montilla's routes offer a journey through culture and history. You can discover architectural gems such as churches, castles, and hermitages. Notable attractions include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5358247" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castle of Espejo&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5316605" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of Fernán Núñez&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20124063" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ducal Palace of Fernán Núñez – Montilla Town Center loop from Montilla&lt;/a&gt; specifically passes by these points of interest.

Q: Are there any natural landmarks or unique ecosystems to explore?
A: Yes, for those seeking natural beauty, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5443523" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna de Zóñar Visitor Center&lt;/a&gt; is near a protected natural wetland, the only natural lake in Andalusia. It's a habitat for over 30 bird species and recognized as a 'Wetland of International Importance,' offering a different natural experience alongside the extensive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4737240" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olive and almond fields&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy the most about road cycling in Montilla?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars. Cyclists often praise the beautiful landscapes of vineyards and olive groves, the varied terrain suitable for different skill levels, and the opportunity to combine cycling with cultural experiences like visiting wineries and historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders?
A: Yes, Montilla offers routes with significant elevation gain for experienced cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1008840905/montilla-moriles-wine-route" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montilla-Moriles Wine Route&lt;/a&gt;, a 65.4 km ride, includes over 730 meters of elevation gain, providing a demanding yet rewarding experience through the region's iconic wine country.
tips: Birthplace of Gonzalo Fernández de Córdoba, the Great Captain, born in 1453 in the castle of Montilla. City famous for the quality of its Pedro Ximénez grape, integrating the region with the Montilla-Moriles Designation of Origin. Passing through the oldest part of Montilla, it is worth visiting the 16th century church of Santiago.

The main economic activity of this municipality is agriculture, especially olive cultivation. According to official information, Nueva Carteya stands out for the lowest income in the province of Córdoba.
3 km of an almost straight road between fields of olive trees. Asphalt in good condition, little traffic.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this road cycling route?
faqs.answer: This moderate road cycling route, covering approximately 57.7 km, usually takes around 2 hours and 50 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for cycling on this route?
faqs.answer: The route is designed for road cycling and primarily follows paved surfaces through the undulating farmland, gentle hills, vineyards, and olive groves of the Montilla-Moriles region. Expect a typical Andalusian countryside experience.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: You'll cycle through the charming settlements of Montilla and Nueva Carteya. A notable highlight is cycling along a Quiet road among olive trees, offering picturesque views of the region's agricultural landscape.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner road cyclists?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as moderate due to its distance and an elevation gain of over 800 meters. While it's on paved roads, it requires good fitness and is generally more suitable for cyclists with some experience rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Montilla-Moriles region experiences hot summers. The best times to cycle are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder and the scenery is vibrant with vineyards and olive groves.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this circular route?
faqs.answer: As a circular route starting from Montilla, you can typically find parking within the town itself. Look for public parking areas in Montilla to begin your ride conveniently.

faqs.question: Are dogs allowed on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While there are no specific restrictions against dogs, it's generally not recommended to bring dogs along on a racebike tour of this nature due to safety concerns with traffic and the sustained pace required.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, this road cycling route does not require any special permits or entrance fees. It utilizes public roads and pathways through the region.

faqs.question: Does the route intersect with any other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, while primarily a road cycling route, it briefly intersects with sections of local paths such as Sendero a Cerro Macho, Camino Huerta de los Frailes, Camino al Cortijo de Frías, Paraje del Cortijo Blanco, Plaza de la Ciudad de Terrassa, and Vereda de la Gamonosa.

faqs.question: What makes this cycling route unique?
faqs.answer: This route offers a unique 'cyclo-enotourism' experience, combining road cycling with the rich wine tradition of the Montilla-Moriles region. You'll pass through extensive vineyards and olive groves, with potential views of traditional wineries, allowing you to appreciate the local culture and landscape.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for refreshments or accommodation along the route?
faqs.answer: As the route passes through settlements like Montilla and Nueva Carteya, you will find opportunities for cafes, restaurants, and potentially accommodation. These towns are good places to refuel or rest.
